data.label: Between two planets by longitude
data.description: Legacy operator testing whether a target astrological object is located between two bounding planets by zodiacal longitude.
data.utility: Low-level geometric test used by advanced conditions such as enclosure, besiegement, or a planet being contained in the zodiacal arc defined by two planets. It only returns a factual yes/no result; interpretation belongs to a higher layer.
data.implementation_notes: Must compare longitudes on a circular 0-360 zodiac. A target at 5 degrees is between bounds at 350 and 20 degrees when the interval crosses 0 Aries. Kept for backward compatibility; new rules should prefer target_between_two_objects_by_longitude when bounds are not necessarily planets.

data.label: Target between two objects by longitude
data.description: Tests whether a target object is located inside the zodiacal arc delimited by two astrological objects.
data.utility: Generic version of the longitude enclosure test. Supports planets, angles, nodes, lots, cusps, fixed stars, or calculated points as target or bounds.
data.implementation_notes: Use circular longitude arithmetic and define whether the tested arc is the direct zodiacal arc, the shortest arc, or an explicitly selected arc. The arc_mode input is mandatory so runtime behavior is deterministic.

data.label: Target outside two objects by longitude
data.description: Tests whether a target object is outside the zodiacal arc delimited by two astrological objects.
data.utility: Complements the between operator for exclusion zones, bounded arcs, and negative geometric conditions.
data.implementation_notes: Should use the same arc-selection convention as target_between_two_objects_by_longitude. The arc_mode input is mandatory so runtime behavior is deterministic.

data.label: Object before object by longitude
data.description: Tests whether one object comes before another in zodiacal order.
data.utility: Useful for relative ordering rules, applying/separating logic, and sequence-based conditions.
data.implementation_notes: The arc_mode input is mandatory so the reference direction and wrap-around at 0 Aries are deterministic.

data.label: Object after object by longitude
data.description: Tests whether one object comes after another in zodiacal order.
data.utility: Useful for relative ordering rules, applying/separating logic, and sequence-based conditions.
data.implementation_notes: The arc_mode input is mandatory so the reference direction and wrap-around at 0 Aries are deterministic.

data.label: Angular distance between objects
data.description: Computes the directed angular distance between two objects on the zodiac.
data.utility: Base operator for aspects, solar distance, lunar phase distance, or any rule depending on a measured separation.
data.implementation_notes: Calculation operator only. It returns a directed angular distance; use compare_number or value_between to turn the computed value into a boolean condition.

data.label: Shortest angular distance between objects
data.description: Computes the shortest angular distance between two objects on the zodiac.
data.utility: Useful for aspects, conjunctions, orbs, combustion-like thresholds, and proximity checks.
data.implementation_notes: Calculation operator only. It returns a value from 0 to 180 degrees; use compare_number or value_between to turn the computed value into a boolean condition.

data.label: Object within longitude orb of object
data.description: Tests whether one object is within a longitude orb of another object.
data.utility: Base proximity operator for conjunctions, angular closeness, solar conditions, and custom near-object rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should normally rely on shortest angular distance unless a directed distance is explicitly required.

data.label: Aspect exists between objects
data.description: Tests whether two objects form a configured aspect within the accepted orb.
data.utility: Low-level aspect detection without assigning interpretive quality.
data.implementation_notes: Should delegate aspect angle and orb thresholds to aspect definition and orb profile tables.

data.label: Aspect angle matches
data.description: Tests whether the angular separation between two objects matches a specific aspect angle.
data.utility: Separates geometric angle matching from orb policy and interpretation.
data.implementation_notes: Use shortest angular distance unless the aspect model requires direction.

data.label: Aspect applying
data.description: Tests whether an aspect between two objects is applying.
data.utility: Supports timing and strength rules without embedding interpretation.
data.implementation_notes: Requires motion direction and relative speeds from the chart calculation layer.

data.label: Aspect separating
data.description: Tests whether an aspect between two objects is separating.
data.utility: Supports timing and strength rules without embedding interpretation.
data.implementation_notes: Requires motion direction and relative speeds from the chart calculation layer.

data.label: Object in sign
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a specific zodiac sign.
data.utility: Base operator for sign placement, dignity, rulership, and interpretation profile rules.

data.label: Object in sign element
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a sign belonging to a given element.
data.utility: Supports elemental distributions and condition profiles without hard-coded sign lists.
data.implementation_notes: Should resolve sign properties through sign profile/reference tables.

data.label: Object in sign modality
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a sign belonging to a given modality.
data.utility: Supports cardinal, fixed, and mutable condition rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should resolve sign properties through sign profile/reference tables.

data.label: Object in sign polarity
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a sign of a given polarity.
data.utility: Supports polarity, sect-related, and temperament-like rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should resolve sign properties through sign profile/reference tables.

data.label: Object in degree range of sign
data.description: Tests whether an object falls within a degree interval inside a sign.
data.utility: Base operator for decans, terms, critical degrees, and custom sign subdivisions.
data.implementation_notes: Degree range is sign-relative from 0 to 30 unless explicitly configured otherwise.

data.label: Object in decan
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a configured decan.
data.utility: Supports decan and face based rules using reference tables instead of hard-coded boundaries.
data.implementation_notes: Should use astral_face_decans or the configured decan reference table.

data.label: Object in term or bound
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a configured term or bound.
data.utility: Supports essential dignity rules based on bounds without duplicating boundary logic.
data.implementation_notes: Should use astral_term_bounds or the configured bound reference table.

data.label: Object in house
data.description: Tests whether an object is located in a specific astrological house.
data.utility: Base house placement operator for accidental dignity and interpretation rules.
data.implementation_notes: House system must be explicit or inherited from chart context.

data.label: Object in house modality
data.description: Tests whether an object is in an angular, succedent, or cadent house.
data.utility: Generalizes object_in_angular_house, object_in_succedent_house, and object_in_cadent_house into one composable operator.
data.implementation_notes: Should resolve modality through house reference tables rather than hard-coded house numbers.

data.label: Object above horizon
data.description: Tests whether an object is above the horizon in the chart.
data.utility: Base visibility and sect operator.
data.implementation_notes: May be derived from houses or from altitude depending on calculation policy.

data.label: Object below horizon
data.description: Tests whether an object is below the horizon in the chart.
data.utility: Base visibility and sect operator.
data.implementation_notes: May be derived from houses or from altitude depending on calculation policy.

data.label: Object near angle
data.description: Tests whether an object is within an orb of a configured chart angle.
data.utility: Generic operator for near Ascendant, Descendant, Midheaven, or Imum Coeli conditions using astral_angle_points.
data.implementation_notes: angle_code should reference astral_angle_points.code.

data.label: Object rules sign
data.description: Tests whether an object rules a given sign under the selected rulership system.
data.utility: Base operator for domicile, house ruler, dispositor, and lordship rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should use configured rulership reference data.

data.label: Object rules house
data.description: Tests whether an object rules the sign on a house cusp.
data.utility: Supports house lord rules without hard-coding sign-to-planet mappings.
data.implementation_notes: Requires house cusp sign and rulership reference data.

data.label: Object in essential dignity
data.description: Tests whether an object is in a selected essential dignity or debility state.
data.utility: Generalizes own sign, exaltation, detriment, fall, triplicity, term, and face checks behind reference data.
data.implementation_notes: Should use astral_object_sign_dignities and related dignity reference tables.

data.label: Object disposes object
data.description: Tests whether one object disposes another by ruling the sign occupied by the second object.
data.utility: Base operator for dispositor chains and dependency rules.
data.implementation_notes: Uses target object's sign and selected rulership system.

data.label: Object motion state is
data.description: Tests whether an object has a specific motion state such as direct, retrograde, or stationary.
data.utility: Generalizes direct, retrograde, and stationary checks for condition rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should use calculated motion state from chart results or motion reference tables.

data.label: Object speed between
data.description: Tests whether an object's speed is within a numeric interval.
data.utility: Base speed operator for fast, slow, station-like, acceleration, or deceleration rules.
data.implementation_notes: Speed unit and sign convention should be explicit.

data.label: Object speed class is
data.description: Tests whether an object belongs to a configured speed class.
data.utility: Supports fast, average, slow, and similar speed rules using reference data.
data.implementation_notes: Should use astral_speed_classes or calculated chart classifications.

data.label: Object distance to Sun between
data.description: Tests whether an object's angular distance from the Sun is within a configured interval.
data.utility: Base operator for cazimi, combustion, under beams, solar visibility, and related conditions.
data.implementation_notes: Keeps solar conditions configurable instead of hard-coding is_combust-like operators.

data.label: Object same sign as Sun
data.description: Tests whether an object is in the same zodiac sign as the Sun.
data.utility: Useful for solar visibility and combustion-family rule variants that require sign context.

data.label: Moon phase between
data.description: Tests whether the Moon phase angle falls within a configured interval.
data.utility: Base operator for waxing, waning, balsamic, quarter, and lunation phase rules.
data.implementation_notes: Usually derived from directed Moon-Sun angular distance.

data.label: Moon is waxing
data.description: Tests whether the Moon is in a waxing phase.
data.utility: Simple lunar phase atom for conditions that do not need an exact phase interval.
data.implementation_notes: Can be implemented as moon_phase_between from New Moon to Full Moon.

data.label: Moon is waning
data.description: Tests whether the Moon is in a waning phase.
data.utility: Simple lunar phase atom for conditions that do not need an exact phase interval.
data.implementation_notes: Can be implemented as moon_phase_between from Full Moon to New Moon.

data.label: Chart is day
data.description: Tests whether the chart is diurnal.
data.utility: Base operator for sect, hayz, and day/night condition rules.
data.implementation_notes: Usually derived from Sun above horizon.

data.label: Chart is night
data.description: Tests whether the chart is nocturnal.
data.utility: Base operator for sect, hayz, and day/night condition rules.
data.implementation_notes: Usually derived from Sun below horizon.

data.label: Object in sect
data.description: Tests whether an object is in sect for the chart context.
data.utility: Atomic condition that can be combined with horizon and sign polarity rules for composed conditions such as hayz.
data.implementation_notes: Should not by itself express the full hayz condition.

data.label: Object out of sect
data.description: Tests whether an object is out of sect for the chart context.
data.utility: Atomic condition for sect-based weakening or mismatch rules.
data.implementation_notes: Should not by itself express a full interpretive judgement.

data.label: All conditions true
data.description: Tests whether every nested condition returns true.
data.utility: Composition operator for building rich astrological rules from atomic tests.
data.implementation_notes: Evaluation can short-circuit after the first false condition.

data.label: Any condition true
data.description: Tests whether at least one nested condition returns true.
data.utility: Composition operator for alternative rule branches.
data.implementation_notes: Evaluation can short-circuit after the first true condition.

data.label: Not condition
data.description: Negates the result of one nested condition.
data.utility: Allows exclusion rules without defining duplicate negative operators for every positive operator.

data.label: Count objects matching condition
data.description: Counts how many objects in a set satisfy a nested condition.
data.utility: Supports rules such as at least three planets in water signs or no personal planet in angular houses.
data.implementation_notes: Usually paired with a comparator in a parent condition.

data.label: At least N objects matching
data.description: Tests whether at least N objects in a set satisfy a nested condition.
data.utility: Convenience boolean operator for threshold-based distributions.

data.label: Exactly N objects matching
data.description: Tests whether exactly N objects in a set satisfy a nested condition.
data.utility: Convenience boolean operator for exact-count rules.

data.label: No object matching
data.description: Tests whether no object in a set satisfies a nested condition.
data.utility: Convenience boolean operator for absence-based rules.

data.label: Compare number
data.description: Tests a numeric value or computed numeric result against another numeric value using a comparator.
data.utility: Turns calculation operators such as angular_distance_between_objects into boolean conditions without mixing calculation and predicate responsibilities.
data.implementation_notes: Use this operator after a numeric calculation operator when the rule needs a threshold comparison.

data.label: Value between
data.description: Tests whether a numeric value or computed numeric result is inside a configured interval.
data.utility: Turns numeric calculation outputs into interval-based boolean conditions, for example testing whether a computed angular distance is within a range.
data.implementation_notes: Use this operator after a numeric calculation operator when the rule needs a range check.
